rationale: The cited paper is used repeatedly as a central prior study, not a passing mention. In the introduction and literature review, the citing paper relies on it to establish that J-shaped review distributions are prevalent and to summarize the dominant utility-based explanation in prior work. The citing paper then explicitly positions its contribution against that interpretation, arguing that reminder effects can also arise from differential attrition and designing analysis to disentangle mechanisms. This indicates a meaningful conceptual role in framing, model development, and contribution positioning.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Schoenmuller et al. (2020) as key prior evidence on review polarity and utility-based explanations, then sharpens that account by introducing differential attrition as an additional mechanism. It refines the prior interpretation of reminder effects by showing they are not attributable to utility differences alone.
